execution_details.stdout:
# /etc/cshrc
#
# csh configuration for all shell invocations.

# Set default umask for non-login shell only if it is set to 0
if ( `umask` == 0 ) then
    umask 022
endif

if ($?prompt) then
  if ($?tcsh) then
    set promptchars='$#'
    set prompt='[%n@%m %c]%# '
    # make completion work better by default
    set autolist
  else
    set prompt=\[$user@`/usr/bin/hostname`\]\$\ 
  endif
endif

if ( $?tcsh ) then
	bindkey "^[[3~" delete-char
endif

bindkey "^R" i-search-back
set echo_style = both
set histdup = erase
set savehist = (1024 merge)

if ($?prompt) then
  if ($?TERM) then
    switch($TERM)
      case xterm*:
        if ($?tcsh) then
	  set prompt='%{\033]0;%n@%m:%c\007%}[%n@%m %c]%# '
        endif
        breaksw
      case screen:
        if ($?tcsh) then
          set prompt='%{\033k%n@%m:%c\033\\%}[%n@%m %c]%# '
        endif
        breaksw
      default:
        breaksw
    endsw
  endif
endif

setenv MAIL "/var/spool/mail/$USER"

# Check if we aren't a loginshell and do stuff if we aren't
if (! $?loginsh) then
    if ( -d /etc/profile.d ) then
        set nonomatch
        foreach i ( /etc/profile.d/*.csh )
            if ( -r "$i" ) then
                if ($?prompt) then
                    source "$i"
                else
                    source "$i" >&/dev/null
                endif
            endif
        end
        unset i nonomatch
    endif
endif

# /etc/cshrc
#
# csh configuration for all shell invocations.

# Set default umask for non-login shell only if it is set to 0
if ( `umask` == 0 ) then
umask 077
endif

if ($?prompt) then
  if ($?tcsh) then
    set promptchars='$#'
    set prompt='[%n@%m %c]%# '
    # make completion work better by default
    set autolist
  else
    set prompt=\[$user@`/usr/bin/hostname`\]\$\ 
  endif
endif

if ( $?tcsh ) then
	bindkey "^[[3~" delete-char
endif

bindkey "^R" i-search-back
set echo_style = both
set histdup = erase
set savehist = (1024 merge)

if ($?prompt) then
  if ($?TERM) then
    switch($TERM)
      case xterm*:
        if ($?tcsh) then
	  set prompt='%{\033]0;%n@%m:%c\007%}[%n@%m %c]%# '
        endif
        breaksw
      case screen:
        if ($?tcsh) then
          set prompt='%{\033k%n@%m:%c\033\\%}[%n@%m %c]%# '
        endif
        breaksw
      default:
        breaksw
    endsw
  endif
endif

setenv MAIL "/var/spool/mail/$USER"

# Check if we aren't a loginshell and do stuff if we aren't
if (! $?loginsh) then
    if ( -d /etc/profile.d ) then
        set nonomatch
        foreach i ( /etc/profile.d/*.csh )
            if ( -r "$i" ) then
                if ($?prompt) then
                    source "$i"
                else
                    source "$i" >&/dev/null
                endif
            endif
        end
        unset i nonomatch
    endif
endif

We have approval. Need to apply with backup, modify. Follow steps: read file, backup, then edit. Use read_file first, then write backup, then modify. We'll read /etc/csh.cshrc.

We need to modify /etc/csh.cshrc to set umask correctly. Currently file contains a block:

```
# Set default umask for non-login shell only if it is set to 0
if ( `umask` == 0 ) then
    umask 022
endif
```

So there is an umask line with value 022 inside conditional. The requirement is to ensure default C shell umask is set correctly. Usually STIG requires umask 077. The recommendation says add or correct the umask setting to read as follows: umask (maybe it's incomplete). We need to set a standard umask, probably 077. We need to ensure there's a line "umask 077" perhaps at top-level, not inside conditional. The best approach: replace any existing umask line (including within condition) with "umask 077". Could comment out conditional? Perhaps just add a line "umask 077" after the conditional, or replace the existing line.

Simplify: use sed to replace any line matching optional whitespace + optional '#' + 'umask' and any characters with "umask 077". But we must ensure we don't replace lines like "if ( `umask` == 0 )". That line contains backticks, but pattern could mistakenly match. Our regex should match lines that start with optional whitespace, optional '#', then 'umask' followed by space and digits, and nothing else after (maybe comment). So pattern '^\\s*#?\\s*umask\\s+.*$' is safe.

We'll apply sed -i -E 's/^\\s*#?\\s*umask\\s+.*/umask 077/' /etc/csh.cshrc

But that will also affect line "if ( `umask` == 0 ) then" because it contains word umask but not at start; it starts with "if". So fine.

We'll backup file first: copy to /etc/csh.cshrc.bak

Then run sed.

After that, verify that there is only one umask line: we can read file again.

Simpler: uncomment (if needed) any umask line outside comments and set value.

We should ensure only one umask line. There is only one umask line currently within if block. We'll replace that line with umask 077. Use sed to replace lines matching optional whitespace then 'umask' and digits. But need to only replace the line within file. Use sed -i -E 's/^\s*umask\s+[0-9]*/umask 077/'.
